questions.q: Who is the founder of the Non-Aligned Movement?
opts: (A) Jawaharlal Nehru
(B) Gamal Abdel Nasser
(C) Josip Broz Tito
(D) Ho Chi Minh
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru was the founder of the Non-Aligned Movement in 1955. He believed in the concept of peaceful coexistence and non-alignment from the Cold War.

questions.q: What is the significance of the 'Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence'?
opts: (A) They were the principles of the Non-Aligned Movement
(B) They were the principles of the Five-Nation Pact
(C) They were the principles of the Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence
(D) They were the principles of the Soviet Union
questions.explanation: The Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence, also known as the Panchsheel agreement, were principles of peaceful coexistence agreed upon by India and China in 1954. They are mutual respect for each other's territorial integrity and sovereignty, mutual non-aggression, mutual non-interference, mutual cooperation for the advancement of each other's economic and social development, and mutual respect for each other's right to live in peace and freedom.
questions.topic: The Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence

questions.q: Who was the Prime Minister of India when India became a member of the United Nations?
opts: (A) Jawaharlal Nehru
(B) Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel
(C) Lal Bahadur Shastri
(D) Morarji Desai
questions.explanation: India became a member of the United Nations on December 30, 1945. Jawaharlal Nehru was the Prime Minister of India at that time.

questions.q: What is the significance of the 'Panchsheel agreement'?
opts: (A) It was an agreement between India and China
(B) It was an agreement between India and Pakistan
(C) It was an agreement between India and the Soviet Union
(D) It was an agreement between India and the United States
questions.explanation: The Panchsheel agreement was an agreement between India and China in 1954. It was based on the Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence.
questions.topic: The Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence

questions.q: What is the purpose of the 'South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation (SAARC)?'
opts: (A) To promote economic cooperation among its member states
(B) To promote political cooperation among its member states
(C) To promote cultural cooperation among its member states
(D) To promote defense cooperation among its member states
questions.explanation: The South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation (SAARC) is an organization of eight South Asian countries. Its main purpose is to promote economic cooperation among its member states.
questions.topic: South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation (SAARC)

questions.q: Who is the founder of the Bangladesh Liberation War?
opts: (A) Sheikh Mujibur Rahman
(B) Indira Gandhi
(C) Zulfikar Ali Bhutto
(D) Ayub Khan
questions.explanation: Sheikh Mujibur Rahman was the founder of the Bangladesh Liberation War. He was a key figure in the struggle for Bangladesh's independence from Pakistan.

questions.q: What is the significance of the 'Simla Agreement'?
opts: (A) It was an agreement between India and Pakistan
(B) It was an agreement between India and China
(C) It was an agreement between India and the Soviet Union
(D) It was an agreement between India and the United States
questions.explanation: The Simla Agreement was an agreement between India and Pakistan in 1972. It was signed by Indian Prime Minister Indira Gandhi and Pakistani President Zulfikar Ali Bhutto.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the 'South Asian Free Trade Area (SAFTA)?'
opts: (A) To promote economic cooperation among its member states
(B) To promote political cooperation among its member states
(C) To promote cultural cooperation among its member states
(D) To promote defense cooperation among its member states
questions.explanation: The South Asian Free Trade Area (SAFTA) is an agreement among the member states of SAARC. Its main purpose is to promote economic cooperation among its member states.

questions.q: Who was the Indian Prime Minister during the India-China war in 1962?
opts: (A) Jawaharlal Nehru
(B) Lal Bahadur Shastri
(C) Indira Gandhi
(D) Morarji Desai
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru was the Indian Prime Minister during the India-China war in 1962.

questions.q: What is the significance of the 'Indus Waters Treaty'?
opts: (A) It was an agreement between India and Pakistan
(B) It was an agreement between India and China
(C) It was an agreement between India and the Soviet Union
(D) It was an agreement between India and the United States
questions.explanation: The Indus Waters Treaty was an agreement between India and Pakistan in 1960. It was signed by Indian Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru and Pakistani President Muhammad Ayub Khan.

questions.q: Who was the Indian Prime Minister during the Bangladesh Liberation War?
opts: (A) Indira Gandhi
(B) Lal Bahadur Shastri
(C) Morarji Desai
(D) Charan Singh
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi was the Indian Prime Minister during the Bangladesh Liberation War in 1971.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the 'Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO)?'
opts: (A) To promote economic cooperation among its member states
(B) To promote political cooperation among its member states
(C) To promote cultural cooperation among its member states
(D) To promote defense cooperation among its member states
questions.explanation: The Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO) is a regional organization that aims to promote economic cooperation among its member states.

questions.q: Who is the founder of the BRICS grouping?
opts: (A) Jawaharlal Nehru
(B) Gamal Abdel Nasser
(C) Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva
(D) Vladimir Putin
questions.explanation: The BRICS grouping was founded by the leaders of Brazil, Russia, India, China, and South Africa in 2009.

questions.q: What is the significance of the 'ASEAN-India Summit'?
opts: (A) It is a summit between India and ASEAN countries
(B) It is a summit between India and SAARC countries
(C) It is a summit between India and the European Union
(D) It is a summit between India and the United States
questions.explanation: The ASEAN-India Summit is a summit between India and the ASEAN countries.

questions.q: Who is the founder of the G20 grouping?
opts: (A) George W. Bush
(B) Vladimir Putin
(C) Angela Merkel
(D) José Manuel Durão Barroso
questions.explanation: The G20 grouping was founded in 1999 by the finance ministers and central bank governors of the world's 20 largest economies.
